chief_complaint: A 72-year-old woman presented with acute substernal chest pain and dyspnea.
history: The patient experienced these symptoms after an acupuncture session. Takotsubo cardiomyopathy is seen after intense emotional or physical triggers.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 100 bpm, RR 24/min. Physical examination revealed decreased breath sounds on the left side.
labs: ECG: anterolateral ST elevations. Chest radiograph: pneumothorax.
